skill.name: gws-gmail
skill.description: Use the Google Workspace CLI to discover Gmail commands and manage authenticated Gmail workflows.
skill.summary: Manage Gmail through documented Google Workspace CLI commands.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command examples, parent-directory links, and weak-crypto patterns. The command findings are legitimate Gmail CLI usage, the parent-directory references are documentation links, and no cryptographic code or prompt injection was found. Risk remains medium because authenticated Gmail commands can read, send, and manage sensitive email data.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Gmail CLI Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run `gws gmail` and `gws schema` commands. This is expected for a Gmail CLI skill, but authenticated commands can read, send, and manage mailbox data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and semantically tied to Gmail account operations. They are legitimate documentation, but the authenticated mailbox scope creates real operational risk.

medium_findings.title: Cross-Directory Skill Dependencies
medium_findings.description: The skill references a shared skill file and helper skill files through parent-directory paths. These references are not arbitrary path traversal, but marketplace packaging should ensure adjacent files are trusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory paths are visible and fixed Markdown references. They are likely safe documentation links, but they intentionally depend on files outside this skill directory.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns on the description and table header lines. No evidence of hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage was found in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain documentation text, not code. There is no semantic evidence of weak cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Static Matches Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick executions are inline Markdown code spans and fenced shell examples. The skill file does not contain Ruby code or dynamic command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation and command guidance. No executable Ruby file or shell interpolation behavior exists in the scanned file.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Gmail CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs use of `gws gmail` commands. This is the intended capability, but it should be used only with an authorized account and user-confirmed email actions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is directly documented and tied to authenticated Gmail access. The concern is data sensitivity, not command injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parent-Directory Documentation References
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses fixed `../` links to shared and helper skill documentation. These are not user-controlled paths, but they expand the review boundary beyond this single file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The paths are fixed Markdown references and do not show traversal of user-supplied input. Review should include referenced files when packaged together.

content.user_title: Manage Gmail with Google Workspace CLI
content.value_statement: Email workflows need accurate command discovery and safe handling. This skill guides Gmail operations through documented Google Workspace CLI commands.
actual_capabilities: Shows the base `gws gmail` command pattern for Gmail API resources and methods.
Guides users to inspect Gmail method parameters with `gws schema` before execution.
Lists helper workflows for sending, replying, forwarding, reading, triaging, and watching email.
Identifies Gmail resource groups such as messages, threads, drafts, labels, settings, and history.
Points users to Gmail profile, watch, and notification stop operations.
limitations: Requires the `gws` command-line tool and a configured Google Workspace authentication context.
Does not include the referenced helper skill implementations inside this single skill file.
Cannot bypass Gmail permissions, organizational policies, API quotas, or user consent requirements.
Email sending and mailbox changes should be reviewed before execution.

use_cases.title: Triage unread email
use_cases.description: Summarize unread Gmail messages and identify items that need a response.

use_cases.title: Prepare customer replies
use_cases.description: Inspect message data and draft replies that preserve Gmail thread context.

use_cases.title: Automate mailbox administration
use_cases.description: Discover Gmail API resources and build commands for labels, settings, drafts, and watches.

prompt_templates.title: Find Gmail help
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the Gmail resources and methods available through the Google Workspace CLI.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a method
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the schema for a Gmail message method and explain the required parameters before running it.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an inbox triage
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me triage unread Gmail messages and present sender, subject, and date before any follow-up action.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Gmail workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a safe Gmail workflow that reads a message, drafts a reply, and asks for confirmation before sending.

output_examples.output: A concise inbox summary with sender names, subjects, dates, and suggested next actions.

output_examples.input: Help me reply to a Gmail thread.
output_examples.output: A drafted response with the target thread identified and a confirmation step before sending.

output_examples.input: What Gmail resources can I manage?
output: Messages, threads, drafts, labels, settings, history, and profile operations.
The skill recommends inspecting each method schema before building command flags.

best_practices: Inspect each Gmail method schema before running a command with mailbox effects.
Confirm recipients, subject, body, and thread context before sending or forwarding email.
Use the least privileged Google account needed for the mailbox task.
anti_patterns: Do not send, forward, delete, or modify email without explicit user confirmation.
Do not paste sensitive email content into prompts unless it is required for the task.
Do not assume referenced helper skills are present unless the local skill package includes them.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps discover and use Gmail operations through the Google Workspace CLI.

faq.question: Does it require authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The `gws` CLI must be installed and authenticated with an account that has Gmail access.

faq.question: Can it send email?
faq.answer: The skill points to Gmail send and reply workflows, but sending should require user confirmation.

faq.question: Can it read Gmail messages?
faq.answer: Yes, when the authenticated account has permission. Users should limit access to necessary messages.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses legitimate CLI commands, but those commands can access sensitive mailbox data.

faq.question: Does the skill contain executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The scanned file contains Markdown documentation and command examples only.
